observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management should conduct one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability by observing the guidelines and procedures afforded in the said Circular which includes but not limited to the following:

Municipal Accountant to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) and reconcile the balance against the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE);

General Services Officer

Maintain Property Cards for PPEs and incorporate the balance in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE).

Identify unserviceable properties and prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for disposal;

Ensure that all equipment/property is assigned with Inventory Tag/Stickers. The property sticker shall contain all vital information of each property/equipment for easy identification such as: a brief description of the property/equipment; model number; serial number; property number; acquisition date; and to whom assigned/responsible Officer/Employee; and

Ensure renewal of Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) every three years or every time there is a change in accountability or custodianship of the property; and

Refer to the Memorandum of Agreement/ Deed of Donation/ Property Transfer Reports (PTR) to determine the cost of donated PPEs. If not provided, coordinate with the Appraisal Committee to assess the fair value of the said PPEs at the date of acquisition in accordance with International Public Sector Accounting Standard (IPSAS) No. 23; and

Inform the resident auditor of the schedule of activities prior to the conduct of the physical count.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following:
The Municipal Treasurer to disburse expenses and other payments of the Local Economic Enterprise (LEE) directly from its designated bank accounts instead of using the General Fund. This ensures that each fund operates independently, preventing unnecessary inter-fund transfers; and
The Municipal Accountant to ensure that all inter-fund transactions are accurately recorded in both the transferring and receiving funds' books and to perform periodic reconciliations of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to prevent discrepancies.

We have recommended to the Municipal Mayor the following:
Designate a separate Special Disbursing Officer for offices that frequently utilize cash advances, to avoid the transfer of accountability, which is prohibited under the rules and regulations governing cash advances
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Require the SDO to submit complete supporting documents (e.g., an estimate or summary of expenses) before allow cash advances in compliance with Section 4.1.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
Ensure that no additional cash advances are granted unless previous cash advance are fully liquidated or properly accounted in strict compliance with Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
Properly monitor the liquidation of cash advances granted to the accountable officers under her jurisdiction; and
Strictly enforce Section 4.1.6 of COA Circular No. 97-002, which prohibits the transfer of cash advances from one Accountable Officer to another.
Direct the SDOs to:
Return/Refund the unutilized cash advances immediately if not used within the allowable period of two (2) months;
Submit liquidation reports immediately after the completion of the activity in compliance with Sections 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
Use the appropriate Report of Disbursements (ROD) Form for liquidating cash advances, following the prescribed format and fund cluster classification.
Require the General Services Officer (GSO), as the SDO, to prepare a detailed annual vehicle registration plan, including estimated costs and payment schedules. This will ensure proper planning and management of cash advances, as vehicle registration can be scheduled in advance based on the expiration dates, thereby preventing unnecessary lump sum advances and ensuring timely liquidation.
Limit the cash advances granted to the GSO as SDO to expenses directly related to its mandated functions. This will ensure proper planning and management of cash advances for transactions where direct check payments are impracticable, minimize unnecessary advances, and enhance financial accountability.

We have recommended to the Municipl Mayor the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Properly classify transactions based on their nature. Expenses related to travel (e.g., per diems, airfare, accommodation) are recorded under Traveling Expenses, not Training Expenses, to maintain accurate financial reporting.
Instruct the LDRRMO to require the barangay to issue an Official Receipt (OR) in acknowledgement of monetary prizes received for proper accountability.

We have recommended to the management the following:
Direct the Municipal Engineer’s Office to:
Issue Certificate of Final Acceptance after one (1) year from completion of the project, provided that the Municipality has no claims filed against the contractor and the surety company. The Certificate of Certificate of Final Acceptance is necessary, as it establishes the starting point of the Warranty Security and the release of Performance Security and Retention;
Establish a monitoring system to track the submission, validity, and expiration of all required securities; and
Require the contractor to post Warrant Securities upon issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance.
Require the Municipal Accountant to refrain from certifying the completeness and propriety of the payment of the final billing to the contractor absent of securities required by RA 9184 and its IRR.

We have recommended to the management the following:
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to coordinate with the Sanguniang Bayan to ensure that all supplemental budgets and realignments are supported by a duly enacted ordinance, reviewed by the Sanggunian Panlalawigan. The ordinance explicitly identifies the new revenue sources or the original source of funds for augmentation, in strict compliance with Sections 305(a), 321, and 336 of Republic Act No. 7160;
Cause the immediate enactment of an appropriation ordinance covering the PPAs funded through the SB resolutions, to correct and provide statutory authority to the already disbursed funds, subject to Sanggunian Panlalawigan review; and
Submit a status report on the action taken and a copy of such ordinance to the Audit Team.

We have recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Engineer and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit all required documents as enumerated in Annex B of COA Circular 2009-001 within the prescribed period or within a reasonable timeframe for all infrastructure projects with Variation Orders. This will facilitate the COA Technical Team's evaluation and ensure compliance with auditing requirements.

We have recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to require the contractor to post a valid Performance Security covering the remaining period until the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance to ensure indemnification in case of non-compliance with the agreed specifications and material quality;
Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that the contractor’s Performance Security coverage complies with Section 39 of                RA 9184 before issuing the Notice to Proceed; and
Review the contractor’s request for a time extension. If unjustified, impose liquidated damages as penalties for project delays.

We have recommended to the management the following:
Direct the Municipal Engineer and project personnel to establish a strict monitoring system to ensure that time extension requests are filed within the prescribed period under Section 11, Annex E of RA 9184 IRR; and
Instruct the Project Inspector to ensure the accuracy and reliability of workability assessments by cross-validating suspension reports with official rainfall data from PAG-ASA. This is to ensure that all work suspensions are fair, justified, and based on verified weather conditions.
It is further recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in the evaluation and approval of the Monthly Time Suspension Reports (MTSRs), ensure that the following supporting documents are present and/or attached to the MTSRs to properly verify and validate the actual number of workable and unworkable days:
Certification from the Municipal Engineer confirming that the pre-determined rainy or unworkable days, as considered in the approved contract duration, have been fully exhausted;
Project Logbook reflecting the actual work progress, weather conditions, and any relevant site occurrences affecting the work schedule; and
Photographs or geotagged photographs submitted by the contractor as proof of unworkable conditions, duly signed and certified by both the Project Inspector and the Municipal Engineer.

Municipal Treasurer

To submit the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due to the Accounting Office to enable the Municipal Accountant to recognize the correct amount of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts at the beginning of the year;

 To ensure that the amounts of delinquent taxes are incorporated in the established receivable amount to be forwarded to the Municipal Accountant, and regularly reconciled with the accounting record; and

Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the procedures provided under the NGAS Manual for LGU in the accounting of RPT/SET and reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer’s records.

Ensure that all equipment/property is assigned with Inventory Tag/Stickers. We recommended to the management the following:
Municipal Treasurer
To submit the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due to the Accounting Office to enable the Municipal Accountant to recognize the correct amount of the RPT and SET Receivable accounts at the beginning of the year;
To ensure that the amounts of delinquent taxes are incorporated in the established receivable amount to be forwarded to the Municipal Accountant, and regularly reconciled with the accounting record; and
Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the procedures provided under the NGAS Manual for LGU in the accounting of RPT/SET and reconcile with the Municipal Treasurer’s records.

We have recommended to the management the following:
The Municipal Treasurer to ensure that its personnel diligently request acknowledgment in the check register from the payee before releasing the checks, and to prepare a schedule of all unreleased checks at year-end for submission to the Municipal Accountant;
The Municipal Accountant to discontinue the practice of categorizing unreleased checks as outstanding checks in preparing its Bank Reconciliation Statements, avoid assumptions and record the transaction/event as it happened; and
The Muncipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher per fund at year-end for the restoration of the unreleased checks. The accounting entry for the restoration of the unreleased check shall be a debit to “Cash in Bank, Local Currency Current” account with credit to the appropriate liability account.

We have recommended to the management the following:
The Municipal Accountant
Appropriately record reversion of obligation caused by stale checks to appropriate liability account instead of lodging it to Other Payables;
Verify the validity of payables aged two years and more and send confirmation letters for balances with identified claimants or creditors. Valid claims shall be paid following the rules and regulations provided in Section 59 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System. Otherwise, cause the reversion of the payables to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund;
Maintain separate FS for each economic enterprise; and
Effect the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify other accounts erroneously booked Other Payables account to ensure that accounts are accurately presented in the financial statements and furnish the Office of the Auditor with a copy of the journal entry voucher.
Propose to the Local Sangguniang to recognize income-generating programs and ventures as Economic Enterprise thru enactment of an ordinance; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payee of the existence of the check one month before it becomes stale. For checks that became stale in the hands of the payee and needs replacement, require submission of the said stale checks prior to issuance of new check.

We have recommended to the management the following:
The Market Supervisor
Through the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant with a Certified List of Market and KTC Stallholders and Delinquent Stallholders as of December 31, 2023 with the amount due to facilitate the recording of accrued rental income, and submit thereafter a Monthly Report of Collection and Balances of each stallholder.
Ensure that all stall occupants are covered by the Contract of Lease based on the rental rates fixed under the Municipal Ordinance 65, s- 2015, also known as “Revised Local Revenue Code of the Municipality of Kapatagan, Lanao del Norte”;
Prepare and maintain Report of Payment and Balances for each Stallholders, and periodically reconcile the same with the Reports maintained by the Treasury and Accounting Office;
Regularly send Billing Statements or Demand Letters to delinquent stallholders to enforce payments; and
Strictly implement the provisions embodied in the contract and Municipal Ordinance 67, s-2015.
The Municipal Accountant
Recognize the total unpaid rentals in the books of the Municipality to the appropriate receivable and income account, and every year thereafter, record the receivable account based on the Certified List of Stallholders with the duly approved Contract of Lease in compliance with the provision of IPSAS 13;
Provide subsidiary ledgers for each stallholder and record the corresponding receivable and income therefrom; and
Maintain a complete file of the approved Contract of Lease.
It is further recommended to management that a copy of the contracts be submitted to COA for auditorial review and evaluation within 5 days from execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-01 dated February 12, 2009.

We have recommended to the management the following:
The Municipal Budget Officer
Ensure that the appropriation for EME in the Annual Budget complies with the provisions prescribed in Section 325 (h) of RA No. 7160 and that the actual expenses sourced therefrom are within the limitation;
Ensure that the appropriation for EME or DF in the Annual Budget is presented under one line item only which is the EME; and
The Municipal Accountant observes the proper use of accounts provided in the COA Chart of Accounts in recording financial transactions.

We recommended that the Management direct the MDRRM Officer to:
Prepare and submit supplemental MDRRMFIP/MDRRMP for all programs, projects and activities (PPAs) to be charged to the MDRRMF which were not yet included in the annual budget. The MDRRMFIP/MDRRMP to be submitted to the Municipal Development Council shall then be the basis of incorporation in the AIP for approval of the Sangguniang Bayan; and
Monitor and control the disbursements sourced from LDRRM Fund Current Appropriation and Unexpended Balance under Special Trust Fund. Ensure that charges made thereof only include those allowable disbursements that relates to Projects/Programs/Activities that support the three thematic areas of LDDRM and should have been incorporated in the approved LDRRM Investment Plan.

We recommended to Management the following measures in accordance with Sections 76 and 77, Title III of RA 7160, COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2020:
Ensure that vacancies on the regular plantilla position of the Municipality are published in accordance with provisions of RA 10741, “An Act Requiring Regular Publication of Existing Vacant Positions in Government Office, Appropriating Funds therefore, and for Other Purposes”;
Prioritize and consider filling up the vacant positions by hiring new employees or expediting the promotion of deserving employees to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the Municipality for the optimal benefit of the constituents as well as to provide equal job opportunities to the community; and
Give priority to the existing Job Order workers in the appointment to the vacant positions provided that such workers meet the appropriate eligibility and other qualification requirements for the position subject to existing Civil Service law and rules.

We recommended the following courses of action to the Local Chief Executive:

Personnel in-charge in the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements shall also be required to submit the list of stale checks issued for more than six (6) months.

b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the accountant with the original Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.
